prompt: Extreme macro photography in a sterile white clinical lab. A continuous, horizontal conveyor belt system is moving. On the belt is a single, uninterrupted topographical surface of human skin, creating a perfect gradient from pristine light skin on the left transitioning to deep, rich dark brown skin on the right. Fixed just above the belt is a single, continuous, horizontal glass-and-metal optical scanning bar. The left half of this scanning bar, positioned over the light skin, is immaculate and crystal-clear, magnifying razor-sharp cellular details beneath it. Exactly at the precise border of skin-tone transition, the glass scanning bar violently shatters. The entire right half of the bar is a chaotic, opaque spiderweb of fractures and cracks, rendering the dark skin beneath it unreadable. Stark, clinical overhead lighting, photorealistic, macro focus, conveying systemic diagnostic blindness and technology failure.
dataset_name: Fitzpatrick 17k (benchmark context for dermatological AI bias)
insight: AI diagnostic algorithms trained on light skin tones fail to accurately interpret conditions on darker skin, creating a literal data barrier that renders marginalized demographics invisible to the machine.
